title: Endostemon kelleri
taxonomy: Endostemon kelleri is classified under the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification places it within the order Lamiales. The genus Endostemon is a part of the tribe Ocimeae. Further classification positions it within the subtribe Ociminae. The plant's classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Endostemon kelleri is a plant species known for its potential medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems are often used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. Further studies may uncover its potential applications in modern medicine.
properties: Unfortunately, I do not have information about the therapeutic properties of Endostemon kelleri.
